response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second, plus Linux and distributed micro-services exposure. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his C/C++ experience is limited and dated (2014-2015 at National Instruments), with recent years focused on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. He has no direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance pipeline experience, so fit for the primary responsibilities is uncertain despite strong adjacent skills.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience as founder of GISCollective and Green Map developer
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux and Kubernetes experience
concerns: Modern C++/Rust experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; recent work is TypeScript/Node
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles are full-stack product work, not large-scale data pipeline design and operation
